prompt:
Tooling ticket: build `rs-tomlite`, a lossless TOML-lite config parser/editor for our deploy tooling. Every existing library deserializes into structs and loses the file layout; we need the opposite — a line-preserving document model our bots can edit surgically, so config diffs stay reviewable. The crate ships with its acceptance suite (tests/parse.rs, tests/edit.rs) and no src/ — `cargo test --offline` fails to compile until you implement the library. Rust 2021, stdlib only. Never touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.

Grammar (deliberately a TOML subset; do not add features): a document is lines, each one of — blank; full-line comment (first non-whitespace char `#`); table header `[name]` where name is dot-separated segments, each segment matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+` (whitespace allowed just inside the brackets, none around dots, nothing but whitespace after `]`); or `key = value` with a bare key `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`. Keys before any header belong to the root table, named `""`. Values: strings `"..."` with exactly the escapes \" \\ \n \t \r; integers `[+-]?[0-9]+` (NO underscore separators); floats `[+-]?[0-9]+\.[0-9]+` only (digits required on both sides of the dot); `true`/`false`; flat arrays `[v, v]` of those scalars (no nested arrays, no trailing comma, `[]` ok). There are NO inline comments: any non-whitespace after a complete value is an error.

Public API (tests import `rs_tomlite::{Document, ParseError, ParseErrorKind, Value}`):
- `Value { Str(String), Int(i64), Float(f64), Bool(bool), Array(Vec<Value>) }` with Debug + Clone + PartialEq.
- `Document::parse(input: &str) -> Result<Document, ParseError>`.
- `ParseError { pub line: usize, pub kind: ParseErrorKind }` (1-based line), Debug + PartialEq, Display `line {n}: {message}`. `ParseErrorKind` with messages: `BadHeader` "malformed table header"; `DuplicateTable { name }` "duplicate table '{name}'" (same header twice, reported at the second); `BadKey` "malformed key"; `MissingEquals` "expected '=' after key"; `BadValue` "unrecognized value" (also for an empty value); `UnterminatedString` "unterminated string"; `BadEscape` "invalid escape sequence"; `DuplicateKey { key }` "duplicate key '{key}'" (same key twice in one table; same key in different tables is fine); `TrailingChars` "unexpected trailing characters".
- `render(&self) -> String` — must reproduce the parsed source BYTE-IDENTICALLY: comments, blank lines, eccentric spacing like `port   =   8080` or `host="a"`, and the presence/absence of a final newline all survive.
- `get(&self, table: &str, key: &str) -> Option<&Value>`; `tables(&self) -> Vec<String>` in document order with root `""` first only when it actually has keys; `keys(&self, table: &str) -> Vec<String>` in document order (unknown table -> empty).
- `set(&mut self, table: &str, key: &str, value: Value)` — the surgical editor. Existing key: rewrite exactly that one line to the canonical form `key = value`; every other line stays byte-identical. New key in an existing table: insert the canonical line directly after that table's last key line (directly after the header if it has none); for the root table with no keys yet, insert at the very top of the document. Unknown table: append at the end — one separating blank line first if the document's last line isn't blank (no padding on an empty document), then `[table]`, then the key line. Canonical value rendering: strings quoted with the five escapes; ints plain; floats always with a decimal point (8.0 renders `8.0`, never `8`); `true`/`false`; arrays `[a, b]` comma-space separated. set() must keep get/tables/keys and a later parse(render()) consistent.

Acceptance: `cargo test --offline` fully green (36 tests: 22 parse + 14 edit). Determinism throughout — no hash-map ordering may leak into tables(), keys(), or any error.
